title: Numbers in investors pitch — emphasize the software aspect (early OSU class pitch)
html:
TL;DR

  • November 15, 2021 micro-note splitting two registers: (1) pitch-prep notes — "bringing a baby for final presentation, EMPHASIZE THE SOFTWARE ASPECT, Maddie Brinkman slides (arch), 3 pint of repetition"; (2) dorm-restock list — Febreze, hand soap, Clorox wipes, utensils, lint roller, highlighters, eyebrow razors, toothpaste, tape.
  • "EMPHASIZE THE SOFTWARE ASPECT" in all-caps — Yahir's earliest written instance of the "the software is the moat" instinct that becomes load-bearing for BroMenu and Rankett. Even at 19 in a freshman class pitch he's already coded software-as-leverage.
  • "bringing a baby for final presentation" — most likely a child-product pitch context (compare with the December child safety company pitch), suggesting these two notes are the same OSU class final.
  • "Maddie Brinkman slides (arch)" names a classmate / pitch-partner — single mention; potential-entity candidate.
  • The dorm-restock half (Febreze, lint roller, eyebrow razors) marks this as still-living-in-OSU-dorms-fall-term — the pitch is for an end-of-term BIS or business class.

Source quote

"-bringing a baby for final presentation
-EMPHASIZE THE SOFTWARE ASPECT
-Maddie brinkman slides (arch)
-3 pint of repiition"

Open questions

  • Is "bringing a baby" literal (someone's actual baby for emotional anchor) or metaphorical (a baby product as a prop)? The December child safety pitch makes literal more plausible.
  • Who is Maddie Brinkman? Single mention here; if she recurs in 2022 OSU notes, promote.
  • Was this for the same business-fundamentals class that produced the December child-safety pitch?

Wikilinks

related.label: Child-safety company pitch fragment + a note for Gabby
